---
title: "Ongoing Research"
editor: visual
---

I am currently working on a few exciting projects. Here are a few of them without much details. If you are interested in any one of them, feel free to reach out to me.

-   U.S. public diplomacy spending: I created a dataset (panel data) with U.S. public diplomacy spending and information on the host countries (trade with the US, trade dependence on Russia and China, terrorist attacks, US troops, UNGA voting similarity with the US etc.). I am currently writing a journal article based on my analysis of this data. I am awarded 2023-2024 Fulbright Visiting Scholar Program to continue to work on this research project at George Washington University while on sabbatical.

-   U.S. overseas missions' budget: I am currently building a dataset (panel data) with U.S. overseas missions' budget as the main variable of interest and information on the host countries (trade with the US, trade dependence on Russia and China, terrorist attacks, US troops, UNGA voting similarity with the US etc.). This dataset is complementary to the aforementioned U.S. public diplomacy spending dataset.

-   Korea's COVID-19 aid. I created a dataset for this research project, and wrote a [working paper for KEI](https://keia.org/publication/south-koreas-public-diplomacy-during-the-covid-19-pandemic-seeking-status-as-an-authority-in-global-governance/) on a similar topic. We presented the preliminary findings at ISA 2023 Annual Conference in Montreal, 2022 International Conference for Public Diplomacy, and Korean Association for International Studies 2022 Annual Conference.

-   Korean high-level diplomatic visits: I created a dataset (panel data) of Korean president and prime ministers' diplomatic visits abroad, and plan to run cross-sectional time-series logistic regression models of South Korean presidents' and prime ministers' diplomatic visits since the country's foundation in 1948. I presented preliminary findings at ISA 2023 Annual Conference in Montreal as well as a workshop organized by *Pacific Affairs* in Toronto in March 2023. This dataset became the starting point of [Korean Diplomacy Data Hub](https://kdiplo.netlify.app).

-   [Public Diplomacy in Other Words](https://pdother.netlify.app/): This is a very exciting project I am organizing for [Journal of Public Diplomacy](https://www.journalofpd.com/). (We are still accepting interested scholars to join the project).

-   Chinese outward FDI: I am co-authoring an article on the drivers of Chinese outward FDI with one my Ph.D. students (Yu Ren).

-   I have a few other paused or upcoming research projects.
